results.question: In what year and city was Nimbus Data Systems founded?
results.expected_answer: In 2017, in Buenos Aires, Argentina.
results.actual_answer: Nimbus Data Systems was founded in 2017 in Buenos Aires, Argentina.

results.question: Who founded Nimbus Data Systems?
results.expected_answer: Valeria Montoya and Andrés Liang.
results.actual_answer: Nimbus Data Systems was founded in 2017 in Buenos Aires, Argentina.
results.verdict_note: Wrong answer: replied with the founding year/city (the previous question's answer) instead of the founders' names. Likely chat-history interference: the founding-year question had been asked 3x earlier in the same thread.

results.question: In which cities does the company have offices, and which one is the headquarters?
results.expected_answer: Buenos Aires (headquarters), Bogotá, and Mexico City.
results.actual_answer: Nimbus Data Systems has offices in Buenos Aires (headquarters), Bogotá, and Mexico City.

results.question: What are the company's four core values?
results.expected_answer: Clarity above all, ownership mindset, technical curiosity, and radical respect.
results.actual_answer:
The company’s four core values are:

Clarity above all
Ownership mindset
Technical curiosity
Radical respect

results.question: Who is the VP of People?
results.expected_answer: Camila Funes.
results.actual_answer: The VP of People is Camila Funes.

results.question: What are the core hours during which the whole team must be available?
results.expected_answer: From 10:00 to 16:00 Argentina Time (ART).
results.actual_answer: Core hours, when the entire team must be available, are 10:00 to 16:00 Argentina Time (ART).

results.question: What stipend do fully remote employees receive?
results.expected_answer: A one-time USD 350 equipment stipend upon joining and a USD 40 monthly internet reimbursement.
results.actual_answer: Fully remote employees receive a one-time equipment stipend of USD 350 upon joining and a monthly reimbursement of USD 40 for internet connectivity.

results.question: How many vacation business days does an employee with 6 years of tenure get?
results.expected_answer: 25 business days (the 5-to-8-year tenure bracket).
results.actual_answer: An employee with 6 years of tenure gets 25 vacation business days per year.

results.question: How many paid leave days are granted for moving (relocation)?
results.expected_answer: 2 business days.
results.actual_answer: Employees are granted 2 business days of paid leave for moving (relocation).

results.question: How far in advance and on which platform must vacation be requested?
results.expected_answer: At least 15 days in advance, through the Humand platform.
results.actual_answer: Vacation must be requested through the Humand platform at least 15 days in advance, and it requires approval from the employee’s direct manager.

results.question: How many unused vacation days can be carried over to the next year, and when do they expire?
results.expected_answer: Up to 5 days, which expire on March 31.
results.actual_answer: Up to 5 unused vacation days can be carried over to the next year, and they expire on March 31.

results.question: When are salaries deposited, and in which months are salary adjustments reviewed?
results.expected_answer: They are deposited on the last business day of each month; adjustments are reviewed in April and October.
results.actual_answer: Salaries are deposited on the last business day of each month. Salary adjustments are reviewed twice a year, in the April and October cycles.

results.question: How large is the annual learning budget per person?
results.expected_answer: USD 800 per year, which can accrue for up to 2 years.
results.actual_answer: The annual learning budget is USD 800 per person per year (and it can accrue for up to 2 years).

results.question: How much is the referral bonus, and what condition must be met?
results.expected_answer: USD 1,500 per successful hire who stays beyond 3 months.
results.actual_answer: The referral bonus is USD 1,500 per successful hire. The condition is that the referred person must stay beyond 3 months.

results.question: What is the daily meal per diem cap for trips to the United States or Europe?
results.expected_answer: USD 90 per day.
results.actual_answer: The daily meal per diem cap for trips to the United States or Europe is USD 90.

results.question: How long is the probation period for new hires, and when is the formal feedback conversation held?
results.expected_answer: The probation period lasts 3 months, with formal feedback at the 45-day mark.
results.actual_answer: The probation period for new hires is 3 months, and the formal feedback conversation is held at the 45-day mark.

results.question: What is the resignation notice period for leadership roles, and for how long can stock options be exercised after departure?
results.expected_answer: 45 days' notice for leadership roles; vested stock options can be exercised for up to 90 days after departure.
results.actual_answer: For leadership roles, the resignation notice period is 45 calendar days. Vested stock options may be exercised for up to 90 days after the departure date.

results.question: Who is the company's CISO?
results.expected_answer: Marcos Ettinger.
results.actual_answer: The company’s CISO is Marcos Ettinger.

results.question: Since what year has the company held SOC 2 Type II certification?
results.expected_answer: Since 2023, renewed annually.
results.actual_answer: The company has held SOC 2 Type II certification since 2023 (renewed annually).

results.question: When is the ISO 27001 certification audit scheduled?
results.expected_answer: For September 2026.
results.actual_answer: The ISO 27001 certification audit is scheduled for September 2026.

results.question: What are the four information classification levels?
results.expected_answer: Public, Internal, Confidential, and Restricted.
results.actual_answer:
The four information classification levels are:

Public — can be disclosed without risk (e.g., website, marketing material).
Internal — company-only access (e.g., process documentation, wikis).
Confidential — access restricted by role (e.g., customer data, source code, contracts).
Restricted — named, audited access (e.g., sensitive personal data).

results.question: What is the minimum required length for corporate passwords?
results.expected_answer: 14 characters.
results.actual_answer: Corporate passwords must be at least 14 characters long. They’re managed through the corporate password manager 1Password.

results.question: What type of MFA must production administrators use?
results.expected_answer: YubiKey physical security keys.
results.actual_answer: Production administrators must use YubiKey physical security keys for MFA.

results.question: Which corporate password manager does the company use?
results.expected_answer: 1Password.
results.actual_answer: The company uses 1Password as its corporate password manager.

results.question: How often is access to systems with Confidential or Restricted information reviewed?
results.expected_answer: Quarterly.
results.actual_answer: Access to systems containing Confidential or Restricted information is reviewed quarterly.

results.question: What is the maximum duration of just-in-time privilege elevation?
results.expected_answer: 8 hours.
results.actual_answer: The maximum duration for just-in-time (JIT) privilege elevation is up to 8 hours through the internal approval system.

results.question: How quickly are accounts deactivated after an employee's departure?
results.expected_answer: Within 4 hours of the departure.
results.actual_answer:
Accounts are deactivated on the employee’s last day as part of the offboarding process (the handbook specifies that the exit interview is held on the last day).
The handbook also states that all equipment must be returned within 10 business days after the departure date, but it does not give a separate “account deactivation” timeline beyond the last-day offboarding step.
results.verdict_note: Not retrieved: expected 'within 4 hours' (security-policy 3.2 Access reviews); the model answered from the employee handbook's offboarding section and said no deactivation timeline was found.

results.question: Where must application credentials, tokens, and certificates be stored, and which tool scans commits for secrets?
results.expected_answer: In HashiCorp Vault; secret scanning runs on every commit using Gitleaks.
results.actual_answer:
Application credentials, tokens, and certificates must be stored exclusively in HashiCorp Vault.
Commits are scanned for secrets on every commit using Gitleaks.

results.question: What encryption is used for data at rest, and what minimum TLS version for data in transit?
results.expected_answer: AES-256 at rest and TLS 1.3 in transit (TLS 1.2 only temporarily for legacy integrations, until December 2026).
results.actual_answer:
Data at rest encryption: AES-256.
Data in transit minimum TLS version: TLS 1.3 (TLS 1.2 is only temporarily allowed for legacy customer integrations and must be eliminated before December 2026).

results.question: What are the RPO and RTO for production databases according to the security policy?
results.expected_answer: RPO of 1 hour and RTO of 4 hours.
results.actual_answer: Production databases have an RPO (Recovery Point Objective) of 1 hour and an RTO (Recovery Time Objective) of 4 hours, as defined in the security policy (Security Policy SEC-001).

results.question: For how many days are production backups retained?
results.expected_answer: 35 days.
results.actual_answer: Production backups are retained for 35 days.

results.question: How long are audit logs from production systems retained?
results.expected_answer: 400 days in immutable storage.
results.actual_answer: Audit logs from production systems are retained for 400 days in immutable storage.

results.question: Which VPN solution is used for remote access, and which MDM manages corporate devices?
results.expected_answer: WireGuard VPN and the Kandji MDM (with automatic lock after 5 minutes).
results.actual_answer: Remote access uses WireGuard VPN with per-device authentication, and corporate devices are managed by Kandji MDM.
results.verdict_note: VPN (WireGuard) and MDM (Kandji) correct; omitted the 5-minute automatic lock detail.

results.question: What is the maximum first-response time for a Critical (S1) security incident?
results.expected_answer: 30 minutes.
results.actual_answer: The maximum first-response time for a Critical (S1) security incident is 30 minutes.

results.question: Within what maximum timeframe must customers be notified of incidents involving personal data?
results.expected_answer: Within a maximum of 72 hours.
results.actual_answer: Customers must be notified of incidents involving personal data within a maximum of 72 hours.

results.question: Which firm performed the 2025 penetration test, and what was the phishing-simulation click rate in the second half of 2025?
results.expected_answer: Carbide Security performed the 2025 pentest; the click rate was 3.1%.
results.actual_answer: The 2025 penetration test was performed by Carbide Security, and the phishing-simulation click rate in the second half of 2025 was 3.1%.

results.question: What is the maximum validity of an exception to the security policy?
results.expected_answer: 90 days, renewable only once, requested in writing to the CISO.
results.actual_answer: An exception to the security policy is valid for a maximum of 90 days, and it is renewable only once.
results.verdict_note: 90 days / renewable once correct; omitted the written request to the CISO.

results.question: What was the Q1 2026 revenue, and what was the year-over-year growth?
results.expected_answer: USD 6.7 million, year-over-year growth of 59.5% compared to USD 4.2 million in Q1 2025.
results.actual_answer: Q1 2026 revenue was USD 6.7 million, and the year-over-year growth was 59.5% (vs. USD 4.2 million in Q1 2025).

results.question: What was the ARR at the end of Q1 2026?
results.expected_answer: USD 26.8 million.
results.actual_answer: ARR at the end of Q1 2026 was USD 26.8 million.

results.question: Who led the Series B round, for how much, and when did it close?
results.expected_answer: Meridian Growth Partners led a USD 40 million Series B, closed in February 2026.
results.actual_answer: The Series B round was led by Meridian Growth Partners for USD 40 million, and it closed in February 2026.

results.question: Which existing investors participated in the Series B?
results.expected_answer: Altavista Capital and Sur Ventures.
results.actual_answer: Existing investors that participated in the Series B were Altavista Capital and Sur Ventures.

results.question: Which product version was launched in February 2026, and what was its main new feature?
results.expected_answer: NimbusVault 3.2, featuring the new hybrid search engine.
results.actual_answer: In February 2026, the company launched NimbusVault 3.2, and its main new feature was a new hybrid search engine.

results.question: How many integrations did the connector marketplace have at the close of Q1 2026?
results.expected_answer: 41 integrations.
results.actual_answer: At the close of Q1 2026, the connector marketplace had 41 available integrations.

results.question: How many active customers did the company have at the close of Q1 2026, and how many were new enterprise customers in the quarter?
results.expected_answer: 627 active customers and 23 new enterprise customers.
results.actual_answer: At the close of Q1 2026, the company had 627 active customers, including 23 new enterprise customers added during the quarter.

results.question: What was the net revenue retention (NRR) in Q1 2026?
results.actual_answer: The net revenue retention (NRR) in Q1 2026 was 118%.

results.question: What was the monthly customer churn in Q1 2026, and how did it change from the previous quarter?
results.expected_answer: 1.8%, an improvement of 0.3 points from 2.1% in Q4 2025.
results.actual_answer: In Q1 2026, the company’s monthly customer churn was 1.8%. It decreased from 2.1% in Q4 2025, a change of -0.3 percentage points.

results.question: What was the NPS in Q1 2026?
results.expected_answer: 56 (up from 51 in Q4 2025).
results.actual_answer: The NPS in Q1 2026 was 56.
results.verdict_note: NPS 56 correct; omitted the comparison (up from 51 in Q4 2025). Same gap as Q47 in the Spanish run.

results.question: What was the quarter's CAC and the LTV/CAC ratio?
results.expected_answer: CAC of USD 11,400 and an LTV/CAC ratio of 4.6.
results.actual_answer: The quarter’s CAC was USD 11,400, and the LTV/CAC ratio was 4.6.

results.question: What is the average annual contract value per enterprise customer?
results.actual_answer: The average annual contract value per enterprise customer was USD 78,000.

results.question: How many active customers does the company have in Latin America?
results.expected_answer: 178 customers.
results.actual_answer: The company has 178 active customers in Latin America (as of Q1 2026).

results.question: What was the gross margin in Q1 2026?
results.actual_answer: The gross margin in Q1 2026 was 74.0%.

results.question: How much cash did the company have at the close of Q1 2026, and what is the estimated runway?
results.expected_answer: USD 19.3 million in cash and an estimated runway of 28 months.
results.actual_answer: The company had USD 19.30 million in cash and equivalents at the close of Q1 2026, and the estimated runway was 28 months (based on the planned investment pace).

results.question: When is the company projected to reach operating break-even?
results.actual_answer: The company is projected to reach operating break-even in Q3 2027.

results.question: What was the annualized voluntary attrition in Q1 2026, and how many active openings are planned for Q2?
results.expected_answer: Attrition of 8.4%; 24 openings are planned, concentrated in Engineering (14) and Sales (7).
results.actual_answer: The company’s annualized voluntary attrition in Q1 2026 was 8.4%. For Q2 2026, it planned 24 active openings (Engineering: 14, Sales: 7).

results.question: What ARR target was set for the close of Q2 2026?
results.expected_answer: Reaching USD 30 million in ARR by 06/30/2026.
results.actual_answer: The ARR target set for the close of Q2 2026 was at least USD 30 million (stated as “ARR ≥ 30 M by 06/30/2026”).

results.question: What percentage of ARR do the top 10 customers represent?
results.expected_answer: 31% of ARR.
results.actual_answer: The top 10 customers represent 31% of ARR.

results.question: What is the production base URL of the NimbusVault API?
results.expected_answer: https://api.nimbusvault.io/v3
results.actual_answer:
The production base URL of the NimbusVault API is:
https://api.nimbusvault.io/v3

results.question: Which technology is used as the API Gateway?
results.expected_answer: Kong.
results.actual_answer: The API Gateway uses Kong (a Kong-based API Gateway routes requests to the ingestion or query services).

results.question: What prefixes do API keys have depending on the environment?
results.expected_answer: nv_live_ for production and nv_test_ for sandbox.
results.actual_answer:
API keys use different prefixes depending on the environment:

Production: nv_live_
Testing (sandbox): nv_test_

results.question: How quickly does API key revocation take effect?
results.expected_answer: In under 60 seconds.
results.actual_answer: API key revocation takes effect in under 60 seconds after you revoke/rotate a compromised key from the panel.

results.question: What scopes can API keys have?
results.expected_answer: Read, write, and admin.
results.actual_answer:
API keys can be scoped for read, write, or admin access.

read: e.g., GET /documents/{id}, POST /search
write: e.g., POST /documents, DELETE /documents/{id}, POST /collections
admin: e.g., POST /webhooks

results.question: How many requests per minute does the Pro plan allow?
results.expected_answer: 600 requests per minute.
results.actual_answer: The Pro plan allows 600 requests per minute.

results.question: What is the daily document ingestion limit of the Starter plan?
results.expected_answer: 1,000 documents per day.
results.actual_answer: The Starter plan has a daily document ingestion limit of 1,000 documents per day.

results.question: What HTTP code does the API return when rate limits are exceeded, and in which headers are the limits reported?
results.expected_answer: It returns 429; limits are reported in X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset.
results.actual_answer:
When the rate limit is exceeded, the API returns HTTP 429.
The limits are reported in these headers:

X-RateLimit-Limit
X-RateLimit-Remaining
X-RateLimit-Reset

results.question: What is the maximum file size accepted by POST /documents?
results.actual_answer: POST /documents accepts files up to 150 MB (for PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, HTML, Markdown, and plain text).

results.question: Which states does a document pass through during processing?
results.expected_answer: queued, processing, ready, or failed.
results.actual_answer: During asynchronous processing, a document moves through these states: queued → processing → ready, or queued → processing → failed.

results.question: What is the default search mode of the POST /search endpoint?
results.expected_answer: hybrid (the available modes are semantic, keyword, and hybrid).
results.actual_answer: The default search mode for POST /search is hybrid.

results.question: What are the default and maximum values of the top_k parameter in searches?
results.expected_answer: Default 10; maximum 100.
results.actual_answer: In searches, top_k has a default value of 10 and a maximum allowed value of 100.

results.question: Which embeddings model does the platform use, and how many dimensions does it have?
results.expected_answer: The in-house nimbus-embed-2 model, with 1,024 dimensions.
results.actual_answer: The platform uses the in-house nimbus-embed-2 embeddings model, and it has 1,024 dimensions.

results.question: For how long are idempotency keys remembered?
results.expected_answer: For 24 hours.
results.actual_answer: Idempotency keys are remembered for 24 hours.

results.question: Which internal code corresponds to an unsupported file format, and with which HTTP code is it returned?
results.expected_answer: NV-1042, with HTTP 422.
results.actual_answer: An unsupported file format corresponds to internal code NV-1042, and it is returned with HTTP status code 422.

results.question: How are webhook deliveries signed, and how many times are failed deliveries retried?
results.expected_answer: With HMAC-SHA256 in the X-Nimbus-Signature header; they are retried up to 8 times with exponential backoff over a maximum of 24 hours.
results.actual_answer:
Webhook deliveries are signed by computing an HMAC-SHA256 signature over the delivery payload using the webhook secret, and sending it in the X-Nimbus-Signature header.
Failed deliveries are retried up to 8 times, using exponential backoff over a maximum of 24 hours.

results.question: How far in advance are breaking changes to the API announced, at minimum?
results.expected_answer: At least 90 days in advance.
results.actual_answer: Breaking changes to the API are announced at least 90 days in advance.

results.question: For how many weeks does the technical buddy accompany a new Engineering hire?
results.expected_answer: For the first 6 weeks.
results.actual_answer: The technical buddy accompanies a new Engineering hire for the first 6 weeks.

results.question: What equipment does an Engineering hire receive on their first day?
results.expected_answer: A MacBook Pro M4 with 32 GB of RAM, already enrolled in the MDM.
results.actual_answer:
An Engineering hire receives a MacBook Pro M4 (32 GB RAM) on their first day, with it already enrolled in the MDM. They also get access via Okta to Slack, Google Workspace, GitHub (nimbus-data organization), Linear, Notion, and Grafana.
They do not get access to AWS and production environments on day 1—that’s enabled after completing security training.

results.question: When is access to AWS and production environments enabled for a new hire?
results.expected_answer: Upon completing the security training (it is not granted on day 1).
results.actual_answer: Access to AWS and production environments is enabled after completing security training—it is not granted on day 1.

results.question: Which versions of Node.js and PostgreSQL does the stack use?
results.expected_answer: Node.js 22 LTS and PostgreSQL 16 (on RDS).
results.actual_answer: The stack uses Node.js 22 LTS and PostgreSQL 16.

results.question: Which tool is used for feature flags?
results.expected_answer: LaunchDarkly.
results.actual_answer:
The indexed sources I found don’t mention which feature-flag tool the stack uses.
If you can point me to the relevant doc (e.g., engineering setup, infrastructure/DevOps docs, or the feature-flag implementation notes), I can answer precisely.
results.verdict_note: Not retrieved: expected 'LaunchDarkly' (onboarding guide, tech stack table); the model said the indexed sources don't mention a feature-flag tool.

results.question: What is the monorepo called, and which tools manage it?
results.expected_answer: nimbus-core, managed with pnpm workspaces and Turborepo.
results.actual_answer: The monorepo is called nimbus-core. It’s managed with pnpm workspaces and Turborepo.

results.question: Which script prepares the local environment, and at which URL does the platform run with pnpm dev?
results.expected_answer: ./scripts/bootstrap.sh; the platform runs at http://localhost:4200.
results.actual_answer:
The local environment is prepared by running ./scripts/bootstrap.sh.
With pnpm dev, the platform runs at http://localhost:4200.

results.question: What format do branch names follow?
results.expected_answer: type/LIN-123-short-description, where LIN-123 is the Linear ticket.
results.actual_answer: Branch names follow the format type/LIN-123-short-description, where LIN-123 is the Linear ticket.

results.question: How many approvals does each pull request require, and what minimum test coverage does the pipeline enforce?
results.expected_answer: 2 approvals plus sign-off from CODEOWNERS, and a minimum coverage of 80% on changed lines.
results.actual_answer:
Each pull request requires 2 approvals plus sign-off from the CODEOWNERS of the affected area.
The CI pipeline enforces a minimum test coverage of 80% on changed lines.

results.question: On which days and until what time are production deployments allowed?
results.expected_answer: Monday through Thursday until 16:00 ART; no Friday deployments except hotfixes approved by the on-call lead.
results.actual_answer:
Production deployments are allowed Monday through Thursday, until 16:00 ART.
They are not allowed on Fridays, except for hotfixes approved by the on-call lead.

results.question: How long are sprints, on which day do they start, and at what time is the daily standup?
results.expected_answer: 2-week sprints starting on Wednesdays; the daily is at 10:15 ART and lasts a maximum of 15 minutes.
results.actual_answer: Sprints are 2-week sprints that start on Wednesdays. The daily standup is at 10:15 ART and lasts up to 15 minutes.

results.question: In which week should a new hire's first pull request be merged?
results.expected_answer: During week 2, with a ticket labeled good-first-issue.
results.actual_answer: A new hire’s first pull request should be merged during week 2.
results.verdict_note: Week 2 correct; omitted the good-first-issue ticket label.

results.question: What is the compensation per on-call week in Engineering?
results.expected_answer: USD 250 per week, plus time off in lieu for overnight incidents.
results.actual_answer: Engineering on-call compensation is USD 250 per on-call week, plus time off in lieu for overnight incidents.

results.question: From what point does a new hire join the primary on-call rotation?
results.expected_answer: Starting in the third month (the first month is spent as a shadow).
results.actual_answer: A new hire joins the primary on-call rotation starting in their third month (after participating as a shadow during their first month).

results.question: When do the internal Nimbus Fridays demos take place?
results.expected_answer: On the last Friday of each month at 15:00 ART.
results.actual_answer: Internal Nimbus Fridays demos take place on the last Friday of each month at 15:00 ART.

results.question: Where are architecture decisions (ADRs) recorded?
results.expected_answer: In the repository, under docs/adr/.
results.actual_answer:
I couldn’t find any indexed documentation stating where Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) are recorded/stored.
The only relevant material returned was a security policy document, which doesn’t mention ADRs or an ADR repository. If you can point me to the engineering process docs (e.g., “architecture process”, “ADR guidelines”, or “engineering handbook”), or tell me what tool they use (GitHub, Notion, Confluence, etc.), I can search again more precisely.
results.verdict_note: Not retrieved: expected 'docs/adr/ in the repository' (onboarding guide §7); the model said it couldn't find ADR documentation and only got security-policy chunks back.

results.question: What is the primary AWS region, and what is the disaster recovery (DR) region?
results.expected_answer: Primary us-east-1 and DR sa-east-1 (São Paulo).
results.actual_answer: The primary AWS region is us-east-1, and the disaster recovery (DR) region is sa-east-1 (São Paulo).

results.question: Which EKS version runs in production, and how is the Qdrant cluster composed?
results.expected_answer: EKS 1.31; Qdrant runs on a 6-node cluster spread across 3 availability zones.
results.actual_answer:
Production runs EKS 1.31.
The Qdrant setup is a 6-node cluster spread across 3 availability zones.

results.question: What is the availability SLA committed to customers, and where is the platform's public status communicated?
results.expected_answer: 99.9% monthly; the status is communicated at status.nimbusvault.io.
results.actual_answer:
The availability SLA committed to customers is 99.9% monthly.
The platform’s public status is communicated at status.nimbusvault.io.

results.question: What is the first-response time for a SEV1 incident?
results.expected_answer: 15 minutes, with a status page update within 30 minutes.
results.actual_answer: The first-response time for a SEV1 incident is 15 minutes.
results.verdict_note: 15 minutes correct; omitted the status-page update within 30 minutes.

results.question: How does a SEV1 incident escalate automatically if it is not acknowledged?
results.expected_answer: It escalates to the Platform lead at 10 minutes and to the CTO at 20 minutes without acknowledgment.
results.actual_answer:
A SEV1 incident escalates automatically via PagerDuty if it isn’t acknowledged:

If not acknowledged within 10 minutes, it escalates to the Platform lead.
If not acknowledged within 20 minutes, it escalates to the CTO.

results.question: On what day and time does the weekly on-call rotation rotate, and when is the secondary paged?
results.expected_answer: It rotates on Mondays at 14:00 UTC; the secondary is paged if the primary does not acknowledge within 5 minutes.
results.actual_answer:
The weekly on-call rotation rotates on Mondays at 14:00 UTC.
The secondary is paged if the primary does not acknowledge within 5 minutes.

results.question: What is the alert threshold for the API's p99 latency?
results.expected_answer: Above 1,200 ms for 5 minutes.
results.actual_answer: The alert threshold for the API’s p99 latency is above 1,200 ms for 5 minutes.

results.question: How long does a full deployment rollback from ArgoCD take?
results.expected_answer: Less than 4 minutes.
results.actual_answer: A full deployment rollback from ArgoCD takes less than 4 minutes.

results.question: When is the scheduled maintenance window, and how far in advance must impactful maintenance be announced?
results.expected_answer: Saturdays from 03:00 to 05:00 UTC, announced on the status page at least 72 hours in advance.
results.actual_answer:
The scheduled maintenance window is Saturdays from 03:00 to 05:00 UTC.
Any maintenance with impact must be announced on the status page at least 72 hours in advance.

results.question: Within what timeframe must the post-mortem of a SEV1 or SEV2 incident be published?
results.expected_answer: Within 5 business days of resolution, in blameless format.
results.actual_answer: The post-mortem for a SEV1 or SEV2 incident must be published within 5 business days of resolution.
results.verdict_note: 5 business days correct; omitted the blameless format.

results.question: How often are Qdrant snapshots taken, and how long did the latest disaster recovery drill take?
results.expected_answer: Snapshots are taken every 6 hours; the DR drill on May 9, 2026 completed full recovery in 3 hours and 12 minutes.
results.actual_answer:
Qdrant snapshots are taken every 6 hours.
The latest disaster recovery drill (run May 9, 2026) took 3 hours and 12 minutes to complete full recovery.
